fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Vermont Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Vermont — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license vermont, dental assistant certification vermont, RDA vermont, DANB CDA vermont, dental assistant radiography vermont

board.name: Vermont Board of Dental Examiners
board.mailing_address: OPR, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Vermont.

steps.title: Create Portal Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the MyLicense Vermont online application portal.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Application
steps.description: Complete the Dental Assistant registration application form through the MyLicense Vermont portal and pay the non-refundable $100 application fee.

steps.title: Provide Personal Documentation
steps.description: Submit proof of age (18 years or older) and proof of high school graduation or its equivalent.

steps.title: Submit BLS Certification
steps.description: Provide documentation of current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification.

steps.title: Demonstrate Professional Qualification
steps.description: Provide proof of graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR provide proof of current Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) certification from the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB).

steps.title: Submit Experience Documentation (if applicable)
steps.description: If applying based on experience in another state, also submit documentation of 3,500 hours of practical clinical dental assisting experience over a two-year period and current CDA certification.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Vermont Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does Vermont require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vermont requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Vermont?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Vermont.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Vermont?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Vermont?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Vermont Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Vermont
hero.dek: Dental Assistants seeking registration in Vermont must apply online through the MyLicense Vermont online application portal, managed by the Office of Professional Regulation.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MyLicense Vermont portal and click 'New User? Create a new account.' Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, email, and set up your password.
